name: POLICY-BACKLOG-14
title: Wave-2 deferred: test-policy-bug-fixes.py restructure + test filename convention pass
description:
Deferred from wave-1 housekeeping (low-priority items, not blocking).

(F5) test-policy-bug-fixes.py is an unbounded kitchen-sink — 9+ already-closed ticket assertions (POLICY-BUG-1/2/7/9/18/19, POLICY-BACKLOG-1/2/5/6/9). Declared deprecation criterion ('when each bug/backlog has its own targeted test or is closed') will never fire since the tickets are already closed. New cleanup waves keep appending indefinitely.

Resolution options:
(a) Split each assertion into its own per-ticket file with concrete EOL.
(b) Restate the file's role as 'regression guard for historical fixes' with real retirement criterion (e.g. 'when structural assertions fold into test-policy-invariants.py').
Lean (b) for size — minimal disruption, just rewrite docstring + add a retirement test that fires when the inner assertions are subsumed.

(F6) Test filename conventions inconsistent across 8 files (5 styles): test-POLICY-1-... (uppercase ID, dashed), test-backlog003.py (no dash), test-backlog-11-12.py (dashed multi-id), test-policy-bug-fixes.py (lowercase, no ID), test-policy-invariants.py (post wave-1 rename), behavior-named files. Pick ONE convention (suggest behavior-first names with ticket IDs as inline comments) and rename to match. Update run.py.

Acceptance: test-policy-bug-fixes.py has a real retirement criterion (or split); all test filenames follow one convention.
